key_points: The story is narrated in the first person by Hari Singh, a young and experienced thief
Hari Singh uses flattery and false names to gain trust and survive
Anil lives a simple life and earns irregularly through writing
Despite being robbed, Anil chooses forgiveness over punishment
Hari Singh realises that education offers respect and security
The story highlights inner conflict and moral awakening
characters: Hari Singh – a clever thief who undergoes moral transformation
Anil – a compassionate writer who believes in reform through trust
themes: Importance of education in personal growth
symbols: Wet fifty-rupee note – Anil’s awareness and silent forgiveness
Railway station – turning point in Hari Singh’s life
exam_tips: Explain how trust influences Hari Singh’s decisions
Mention education as a tool of reform
Link symbols to Hari Singh’s inner conflict
Support answers with incidents from the story
